{{Short description|2010 Italian film festival}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2020}} {{Infobox Film festival |name = 67th Venice International Film Festival |image = VeniceFilmFestival67poster.jpg |image_alt = |caption = Festival poster |opening =''[[Black Swan (film)|Black Swan]]'' |closing =''[[The Tempest (2010 film)|The Tempest]]'' |location = [[Venice]], [[Italy]] |founded = 1932 |awards = [[Golden Lion]]:
''[[Somewhere (film)|Somewhere]]'' |artistic_director= Marco Müller |host = [[Isabella Ragonese]] |number = |date = 1 – 11 September 2010 |website = [http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/ Website] | chronology = Venice Film Festival | preceded_by = [[66th Venice International Film Festival|66th]] | followed_by = [[68th Venice International Film Festival|68th]] }} The '''67th annual [[Venice Film Festival|Venice International Film Festival]]''', was held from 1 to 11 September 2010, at [[Venice Lido]] in [[Italy]].{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/festival/lineup/ |title=67th Venice International Film Festival Line-up |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100801004851/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/festival/lineup/ |archive-date=1 August 2010 |access-date=29 July 2010 }} American filmmaker [[Quentin Tarantino]] was the jury president for the main competition.{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/10101071 |title=BBC News: Quentin Tarantino to lead Venice film jury |work=BBC News |url-status=live | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100509190745/http://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/entertainment_and_arts/10101071.stm| archive-date= 9 May 2010}} The [[Golden Lion]] was awarded to ''[[Somewhere (film)|Somewhere]]'' by [[Sofia Coppola]].{{cite web |title=Venezia 67 Awards |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/venezia67-awards.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100913153106/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/news/venezia67-awards.html |archive-date=13 September 2010 |work=labiennale.org}}{{cite news |date=11 September 2010 |title=Sofia Coppola wins Venice Golden Lion for 'Somewhere' |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-11273842 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100912125739/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-11273842 |archive-date=12 September 2010 |work=[[BBC News]] |publisher=[[British Broadcasting Corporation]]}} Chinese-Hong Kong filmmaker [[John Woo]] received the [[Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement]] during the festival.{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/woo.html |title=John Woo to receive Golden Lion award in Sept. 2010 |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100408105239/http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/woo.html |archive-date=8 April 2010 |access-date=17 June 2010 }} In a break with tradition of limiting a film to receiving no more than one major award, the [[Special Jury Prize (Venice Film Festival)|Special Jury Prize]] and the [[Volpi Cup for Best Actor]] ([[Vincent Gallo]]) went to the same film, [[Jerzy Skolimowski]]'s ''[[Essential Killing]]''. In the past, no one film had been given two major awards. Representing the jury, American director [[Quentin Tarantino]] appealed to Festival head [[Marco Müller (director)|Marco Müller]] to alter the rules. This rule change continues to be upheld for future editions of the Festival.{{cite web |url=http://www.iofilm.co.uk/awards/essential-killing-takes-triple-at-venice |title=Essential Killing Takes Triple at Venice |work=inside out film |url-status=dead |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100921141745/http://iofilm.co.uk/awards/essential-killing-takes-triple-at-venice| archive-date= 21 September 2010}}{{cite news|url=http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-world/coppola-wins-venice-filmfests-golden-lion-for-somewhere-20100912-156b2.html |title=Coppola wins Venice filmfest's Golden Lion for 'Somewhere' |last=Doggett |first=Gina |date=12 September 2010 |publisher=[[Sydney Morning Herald]] |work=[[Fairfax Media]] |url-status=dead | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100915190544/http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-world/coppola-wins-venice-filmfests-golden-lion-for-somewhere-20100912-156b2.html| archive-date= 15 September 2010}} Following the Festival, Italian film critic [[Paolo Mereghetti]] criticized the decisions that the jury made in awarding prizes, and singled out Tarantino, accusing him of favoritism, which he denied.{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-11281250 |title=Quentin Tarantino denies Venice nepotism claim |access-date=13 September 2010 |work=BBC News | date=7 May 2010 |url-status=dead | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100913155710/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-11281250| archive-date=13 September 2010}}{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2010/sep/14/tarantino-favouritism-claim-venice |title=Quentin Tarantino accused of favouritism in Venice film festival awards |work=guardian.co.uk |url-status=dead | date=15 September 2010| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100917111429/http://www.guardian.co.uk/film/2010/sep/14/tarantino-favouritism-claim-venice| archive-date= 17 September 2010}} The festival opened with ''[[Black Swan (film)|Black Swan]]'' by [[Darren Aronofsky]], and closed with ''[[The Tempest (2010 film)|The Tempest]]'' by [[Julie Taymor]].{{cite journal | last=Lyman | first=Eric J. | url=http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/hr/content_display/film/news/e3i4c15c030a696fa142f87203f980fdbe3 | title=Aronofsky's 'Black Swan' to open Venice fest | journal=[[The colspan="4" |Lux Prize |- |''Plato‘s Academy'' |''Akadimia Platonos'' |[[Filippos Tsitos]] |Greece, Germany |- |''[[When We Leave]]'' |''Die Fremde'' |[[Feo Aladag]] |Germany |- |''[[Illegal (2010 film)|Illegal]]'' |''Illégal'' |[[Olivier Masset-Depasse]] |Belgium, Luxembourg, France |} ==Official Awards== Starting with the 67th edition of the festival, four new awards were established for the ''Orizzonti'' section: the Orizzonti Award (for feature films), the Special Jury Orizzonti Prize (for feature films), the Orizzonti Award for Short Experimental Films and the Orizzonti Award for Short/Medium-length Films.{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/orizzonti-2.html |title=Four new "Orizzonti" awards |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100326132903/http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/orizzonti-2.html |archive-date=26 March 2010 |access-date=26 April 2018 }} The [[Golden Lion]] was won by ''[[Somewhere (film)|Somewhere]]'', directed by [[Sofia Coppola]], a film based in part on Coppola's childhood as the daughter of acclaimed American director [[Francis Ford Coppola]]. [[Quentin Tarantino]], the president of the jury that awarded the prize, hailed the film saying, "it grew and grew in our hearts, in our minds, in our affections".{{cite news |date=11 September 2010 |title=Coppola wins Venice filmfest's Golden Lion for 'Somewhere' |url=http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-world/coppola-wins-venice-filmfests-golden-lion-for-somewhere-20100912-156b2.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100915190544/http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-world/coppola-wins-venice-filmfests-golden-lion-for-somewhere-20100912-156b2.html |archive-date=15 September 2010 |work=[[Sydney Morning Herald]] |publisher=[[Fairfax Media]]}} The jury's decision was unanimous. Upon receiving the award, Coppola paid credit to her father for "teaching me".{{cite news |last=Nayeri |first=Farah |date=12 September 2010 |title=Sofia Coppola's 'Somewhere' Wins Golden Lion Top Prize at Venice Festival |url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/2010-09-11/sofia-coppola-s-somewhere-wins-at-venice-film-festival.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100913141134/http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2010-09-11/sofia-coppola-s-somewhere-wins-at-venice-film-festival.html |archive-date=13 September 2010 |work=bloomberg.com |publisher=[[Bloomberg L.P.]]}} The Russian film ''[[Silent Souls]]'' and the Chilean film ''[[Post Mortem (2010 film)|Post Mortem]]'' had been considered favorites for the award.{{cite news |date=11 September 2010 |title=In Venice, Russian, Chilean films favoured for Golden Lion |url=https://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5gAkQLnUmXKklmBObPVEVxQlpzTbA |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130124191447/http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5gAkQLnUmXKklmBObPVEVxQlpzTbA |archive-date=24 January 2013 |access-date=11
